page: Amazon Web Services r7i.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring the x86_64 architecture. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor operating at 3.2 GHz, delivering 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall within the average range, supported by a large 105 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. However, multi-core performance is weak to poor, as evidenced by the low multi-core Geekbench and stress-ng results, which is expected given the single physical core limitation. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for large uncached blocks are also poor, registering around 11 GB/sec, while cached small-block reads perform at a weak 151.29 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7i.large offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. Tradeoffs include low multi-threaded performance, poor database operation benchmarks, and weak Redis SET performance. This instance is best suited for light database caching, basic text processing, single-threaded scripting, and low-throughput web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over compute density.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.large is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs (1 physical core), 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM at 5 GHz, and a 1 Gbps baseline network speed, with no local storage or GPU. While single-core performance benchmarks like Geekbench and stress-ng are average, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are weak to poor due to the single physical core constraint. The instance supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Qualitatively, the r7i.large provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications. It is suited for workloads such as light database caching, text processing, and basic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering average single-core but weak multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.large is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a dedicated Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring DDR5 RAM and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio)
Average single-core performance with weak multi-core scaling
Supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instruction sets
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for light database caching and text processing
